content: We can reframe underutilized Soft Q-Learning as an implicit curiosity regulator for 2025 ICM-powered autonomous swarm agents, where Soft Q-Learning's inherent maximum entropy objective naturally amplifies ICM's intrinsic reward signal to prevent the swarm from getting stuck in repetitive, low-discovery local optima. This combination addresses a key unaddressed flaw of current ICM swarm systems: over-specialized curiosity that wastes exploration bandwidth on trivial redundant targets instead of exploring high-value uncharted environments.
